---
title: "ITensor"
format: html
engine: julia
execute:
  daemon: false
julia:
  exeflags: ["--project=@."]
---

# purity

## classical shadows

### cases

```{julia}
using RandomMeas
# U
N = 4
NU = 200
measurement_settings = [LocalUnitaryMeasurementSetting(N) for _ in 1: NU];
```

```{julia}
# state
site_indices = siteinds("Qubit",N);
ψ = random_mps(site_indices, linkdims=2); # Quantum state of interest
```

```{julia}
# measure and get the data
NM = 10 # Number of shots per setting 
measurement_group = MeasurementGroup(ψ , measurement_settings, NM);
```

```{julia}
# create shadows
factorized_shadows = get_factorized_shadows(measurement_group);
dense_shadows = get_dense_shadows(measurement_group);
```

```{julia}
@elapsed get_trace_moment(dense_shadows, 2) # 4.676559065
```

```{julia}
@elapsed get_trace_moment(factorized_shadows, 2) # 137.674895947
```

### Performance analysis

* dense_shadows: The time complexity is $O(NU^2)$ . However, it has high space requirements, so it is suitable for small systems.

* factorized_shadows: The time complexity is $O(NU^2NM^2)$ . However, it has lower space requirements, so it is suitable for biger systems; The structure is suitable to parallel computing **but which could not use @Threads out of get_trace_moment because cross term, you must write a new method**.
